subject: 🏃 Tuesday — Rest from running + Upper Push
html:
Today: No running. Knee went Yellow yesterday afternoon after Monday's Lower A — exactly the call we built into Week 1. Upper Push today, which is legs-off and catches up the push session you skipped Monday. Clean win.

Dynamic warm-up (~5 min, rep-based): 10 leg swings forward/back each leg, 10 lateral leg swings each leg, 10 bodyweight squats, 10 hip circles each direction, 10 glute bridges, 10 ankle circles each direction.

Upper Push — 3 rounds:

ExerciseWeightSets × Reps
Dumbbell bench press30 lbs/hand3×10
Overhead press (dumbbell)20 lbs/hand3×10
Push-upsBodyweight3×12–15
Dumbbell lateral raise10 lbs3×12
Tricep overhead extension20 lbs3×10

Focus cue: Leave 2–3 reps in reserve on every set. Upper body is pure maintenance for 15 weeks — no chasing PRs, no going to failure. Spend nothing extra here.

Context: Week 1 of 15, Base phase, 14 weeks to race day. The entire job of these first two weeks is confirming the knee tolerates consistent load before tempo work starts in Week 3 — not building fitness.

Adaptation notes: Yellow after Day 1 is expected — your right leg hasn't done controlled single-leg work in months, and the asymmetry you felt on SL squats and step-downs is the exact imbalance we're fixing. Ice the knee tonight, keep moving with walks, don't sit all afternoon. Report back tomorrow: if the knee is back to Green by Wednesday night, Thursday's easy 5 mi is on; if still tingly, we cut to 3 mi or push to Friday.
